diagrams.title: Pointer Variable and Memory Address
diagrams.description: Shows how a pointer variable stores a memory address and points to another variable. Demonstrates the relationship between a regular variable, a pointer variable, and the memory address it holds.
elements: Regular variable box with name and value
Pointer variable box showing address value
Arrow from pointer to pointed variable
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental concept of pointers: variables that store memory addresses instead of actual values

diagrams.title: Dynamic Memory Allocation Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Illustrates the complete lifecycle of dynamic memory: malloc() allocating memory on the heap, pointer storing the address, using the allocated memory, and free() releasing the memory back to the system.
diagrams.purpose: Teaches the process of dynamic memory allocation and deallocation, preventing memory leaks in C programs

diagrams.title: Stack vs Heap Memory Model
diagrams.description: Shows the memory layout of a C program with the Stack (containing local variables and function calls) and Heap (containing dynamically allocated memory). Demonstrates how pointers can reference both stack and heap memory.
elements: Heap region with allocated memory blocks
Arrow from stack pointer to heap memory
Arrow from stack pointer to stack variable
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the difference between stack (automatic) and heap (dynamic) memory, and how pointers can reference either region
